title: eleocharis quinqueflora subsp. suksdorfiana
intro: Eleocharis quinqueflora subsp. suksdorfiana is a subspecies of the Eleocharis quinqueflora species, commonly known as Suksdorf's spike-rush, a type of sedge plant native to North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Suksdorf's spike-rush include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
